text: Belaa Rebɛka ŋɔ Aesek be ŋɔ, “N sie aa Hiti bitoli nɔ nɛɛ lɛ. N kii ba kɔkɔkɔ. Dɛ Jekɔb ŋɔ wɛɛ mo ba bini bɛlee yaa, mɛɛ sewɔɔ hɛo.”
translated: When Rebekah said to Isaac, "I am weary of my life because of the Hittite women. If Jacob takes a wife from the daughters of Heth like these, what good will my life be to me?"

ref: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
text: Asɛ ɔmee lawɔ o ŋɔ, “Bonɔ ɔma kɛrɛdɛ! Bonɔ ɛɛ ɛ Korowii demii. Bonɔ ɛ doonyoa baa kpɔ o ta dɛɛ kaa nyundua.”
translated: Then the woman said to him, "You are a liar! You are not Korowi's child. You are just a poor person who has come to deceive us."

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: Asɛ Jekɔb ŋɔ ba be ŋɔ, “Wee ta nyɛɡre he ba boa peera nɔ nyoa la, hé tɛa nee a nyɔ dɛ berɛ kpɔa kaa dibɔr.”
translated: And Jacob said to them, "My brothers, where are you from?" And they said to him, "We are from Haran."

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Belaa ba chaŋe ŋɔ, “Yaa won ɛ ɡɛ, nemenɛ a bwa ba boa nyoa bonɔ, ásɛ ya ɡbili bwe nɔ le kɔlɔŋ nɔ nyoa mɔɔ dɛ́ tɛa nee a nyɔ.”
translated: They answered him, "Are you also from Galilee? Search and see that no prophet arises from Galilee."

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Tɔpere la aa tela la, Jekɔb ŋɔ Laban be ŋɔ, “Bene anuanɛ la telo, tɛ mɛ n haah la ásɛ n mo name.”
translated: In the morning, Jacob said to Laban, "Send me away so that I can go to my own homeland."

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Kɛ a jwaana la, Leaa Laban berɛ lawɛ ba tɛ Jekɔb asɛ o ne o ka chɔ.
translated: When he heard that Laban's sons were saying that Jacob had taken all that belonged to their father.

ref: and they had a son.
text: Asɛ o kpoŋe tea dɛ lolɛ bibaa tɛ Jekɔb.
translated: The woman who gave birth to the children of Jacob.

ref: but Leah said, “It's bad enough that you stole my husband! Now you want my son's love flowers too.” “All right,” Rachel answered. “Let me have the flowers, and you can sleep with Jacob tonight.”
text: Asɛ Lea ŋɔ, “Lɛɛ haa lɛ n jaa nɔ ɡbo ta maɡra aa, he berɔ dɛɛ nyin he lɛ n bie kwaa nɔ o be ja aa?” Anee Rakɛl ŋɔ, “Dɛ hɛɛ tɛ mɛ he bie kwaa nɔ anee yaa, mɛɛ sɛoh dɛ tɛ he ne Jekɔb kalaŋre jwaana nɔ ne.”
translated: Then Leah said, "Is it a small thing that you have taken my husband, and now you want to take my son's mandrakes also?" And Rachel said, "Therefore he shall lie with you tonight in exchange for your son's mandrakes."

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: Anomanta, o lolɛ bibaa dɛ ŋɔ, “Korowii tɛ mɛ bibaa dɛ laa sihiih kpɔ n sie.”
translated: "Anomanta, he said to his wife, "Korowii is my wife's younger sibling so I will marry her."

ref: But Laban told him, “If you really are my friend, stay on, and I'll pay whatever you ask. I'm sure the Lord has blessed me because of you.”
text: Gel n jemɛ kembɔr maa chɛ kɛ n kpɔ tɛɛ, ásɛ n nyin tɛɛ.”
translated: "Go and tell the elders that I have seen the matter, and I know the matter."

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Jekɔb pere a bii la le anɔ la ta, dɛ ɡel anɔ la aa kaa la sie chaɡɛ Laban kwaa la waa paaɡe daa a sie la ne ambiŋere la be. Asɛ dɛ teɡɛ o ɡbabir kwaa la kaa baŋ bɛl, o ta an kpɔe koo Laban kwaa la ta.
translated: Jacob took his wives and children and all that he had acquired, and he fled with all his possessions from Laban without telling him. So he crossed the river with all his belongings and set out toward the hill country of Gilead.

ref: Once, when the flocks were mating, I dreamed that all the rams were either spotted or speckled.
text: Peera la teene kpoŋa saŋ la, n deero dɛ na kɛŋɔ bombala aa dɛ jweera la peeɡɔ dɛ lɛɡre.
translated: When the child was crying, his mother gave him a sweet so that he would stop crying.

ref: long years to earn your two daughters and another
text: Jekɔb ta ɡelɛ Laban jemɛ kɛŋɔ o dɛ kaa o bwɛ.
translated: Jacob took Laban's daughter and went to his home.

ref: and said: Look what you've done! You've tricked me and run off with my daughters like a kidnapper.
text: Laban la Jekɔb be dɛ ka ŋɔ, “Ɛrɛɛ he ɛɛ mɛ ɡɛ nɔɔ? He neɡ mɛ dɛ kpɔ n bihaahna chwa kɛŋɔ haa kaa yo laa dɛ laɡ ba la.
translated: Then Jacob became angry and said, "What is my offense? What is my sin that you have hotly pursued me?"

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Dɛ berɛ ŋɔ, “Dɛ he ka dɛ fiiɡi n bihaahna nɔ kɛɛ dɛ ka mo haahna ja ba nɛ aa, a lole kɛŋɔ maa belaa do te, kɛ Korowii te ɛɛ nao.
translated: At that time, "If we do not repent and turn from our evil ways, it will be very bad for us, because God is coming.

ref: Early the next morning, Laban kissed his daughters and his grandchildren goodbye, then he left to go back home.
text: Tɔ aa pera chɔmbibi la, Laban mo o bihaahna ne o naanar la lwɛrɛme, dɛ swee andɛɛɡa tɛɛ ba dɛ berɛ kaa o bwɛ.
translated: When Laban heard the news about his sister's son Jacob, he ran to meet him and embraced him and kissed him and brought him to his house.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Belaa Jekɔb tomɛ toroma ŋɔ bá tɔ o sie dɛ́ kaa o dɔɔ Esɔ be. Dɛ Esɔ soɡe harɛ bɛl baa dɛ yerɛ bela Edom.
translated: Before Jacob's descendants became a nation, his brother Esau also had descendants. Esau later became known as Edom.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: Kondɛɛra waa peɡri a bini la, o ŋɔ bá kpɔ ka tɛ o dɔɔ, a bɛlbwa kapa ne nɛn waa dɛ nyiŋa. Asɛ dɛ ŋɔ o bonɔɔna la be ŋɔ, “Hé tɔ n sie, ásɛ dɛ ɡel he baahna tɛr.”
translated: When the hyena saw that the child was about to fall, he quickly ran and caught the child with his mouth. Then he said to his friend, "Let's go home, because the sun is getting very hot."

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekɔb chiɡ ŋwɛnɛ ta dɛɛ kaa, asɛ nyundua toroma ba chemo.
translated: Jacob also married her, and he loved Rachel more than Leah.

ref: He thought, “If Esau attacks one group, perhaps the other can escape.”
text: O liiro kɛŋɔ dɛ Esɔ ba dɛ yo baŋ bɛl nɛra aa, ba kar la ɛɛ won dɔ bɔr dɛ́ chwa kaa.
translated: When Esau's wives saw that they did not please his father, they went and took other wives in addition.

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Jekɔb chɔ bela awola jwaana la bwa, asɛ dɛ chale o kondɛɛra la bini ane dɛ́ kpɔ ka tɛ Esɔ.
translated: Jacob took his two wives and his children and all his possessions and crossed over to go and meet Esau.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Hé berɛ ɡel ó jemɛ kɛŋɔ o bonɔɔ Jekɔb ɡe lɔ habɔɔ dɛɛ ba. Dɛ ń tɔ sie, dɛ́ kpɔ koŋkɛɛla nɔ ba tɛɛ, ásɛ mɔɔ o tel he be. Ásɛ dɛ he nao waa, he ken ɛɛ kpɔɔ kɛɔ.”
translated: At that time when your father Jacob was still alive, he told us. He said that we should tell you this, that he said it before he died. He said we should say this, that you should forgive the sin.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Belaa Jekɔb tomɛ toroma ŋɔ bá tɔ o sie dɛ́ kaa o dɔɔ Esɔ be. Dɛ Esɔ soɡe harɛ bɛl baa dɛ yerɛ bela Edom.
translated: Before Jacob's descendants became a nation, his brother Esau had already established the nation of Edom.

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Jekɔb aa no ɡɛ la, o ɔmɔ kohona o bwara yɔr, asɛ dɛ bɔne o nɛra la ne peera ne nae, dɛ nyoɡmare la bini bɔre anɛ.
translated: When Jacob saw him, he tore his clothes in grief, because he loved him more than all his other children, and mourned for him many days.

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: he chaŋe ŋɔ, ‘He bonɔɔ Jekɔb kwaa. O lawa ŋɔ ń kpɔ ka tɛ o dɔɔ Esɔ. O ɡe lɔ mun ta dɛɛ ba.’ ”
translated: He said, "He is no longer called Jacob. His name that I have given him is Israel. He has fought with God and with people and has won."

ref: Meanwhile, Jacob heard what had happened. But his sons were out in the fields with the cattle, so he did not do anything at the time.
text: Dɛ Jekɔb noɔ kɛŋɔ Sɛkem ɡuɡri o bihaah Daena o ne o chɔ, kɛ bela la, o ta wobɛlbwa chaŋɛ, dɛkalkɛ dɛ o bibaala la kaa nae ne dibɔr ba ta balɛ mɔɔ.
translated: When Jacob arrived safely at the city of Shechem in the land of Canaan, on his journey from Paddan Aram, he camped within sight of the city.

ref: just as Jacob's sons were coming in from work. When they learned that their sister had been raped, they became furiously angry, because nothing is more disgraceful than rape, and it must not be tolerated.
text: dɛ Jekɔb bii la ɡe denɔ le kpɔɡ bini ba. Baa baa no waa la la, a we ba nɛ, asɛ ba baah di kohona kɛŋɔ Sɛkem aa ɛ wedoŋa dɛ nyiŋi Israel tena nyubaah kɛŋɔ waa ɡuɡri ba nimambie o ne o chɔ la.
translated: And Jacob's sons came from the field when they heard it. The men were grieved and very angry, because he had done a disgraceful thing in Israel by lying with Jacob's daughter, for such a thing must not be done.

ref: Why don't you start letting your families marry into our families and ours marry into yours?
text: Gel ya nyoa pɛ yaa mo he bihaahna, ásɛ he ɡe ɛɛ mo ya kwa.
translated: You should not be afraid of him, because he is your friend.

ref: They answered, “Was it right to let our own sister be treated that way?”
text: Anee ba chaŋe ŋɔ, “Yaa won so nyiŋi ba ɛ ya nimambie kɛŋɔ hanjwamane.”
translated: "If you do not do this, 'You will sin against God and he will punish you.'"

ref: While Jacob and his family were traveling through Canaan, God terrified the people in the towns so much that no one dared bother them.
text: Jekɔb ne o bii aa dɛ ba bela ira la, Korowii ɡelɔ ɔmee kohona chole nɛra baa lɔ bwɛɛre aa bɛne bela tena bwa nɛ ba ta wone ti ba dɛ ɛɛ ba wobɛlbwa.
translated: Jacob and his children were living there, in the land of Canaan where his father had stayed as a foreigner and they were doing the work that their father used to do.

ref: During their time there, Jacob's oldest son Reuben slept with Bilhah, who was one of Jacob's other wives. And Jacob found out about it. Jacob's Twelve Sons
text: Waa kaa so harɛ la ta la, Rubin kaa lawɛ tolo baa dɛ yerɔ Bilha baa lawa tɛ o mɛɛ o ɛ o sɛno la chɔ, asɛ Jekɔb no. Bii fi dɛ banɛɛ Jekɔb lolɛ.
translated: While they were living there, Reuben went and slept with his father's concubine Bilhah, and Israel heard about it. Jacob had twelve sons.

ref: He did this because the land was too crowded and could not support him and his brother with their flocks and herds.
text: Dɛkalkɛ dɛ harɛ o ne Jekɔb aa soɡe o ta la aa kanɛ tɛɛ ba. Ba dɔ kondɛɛra asɛ a ya, anomanta, dɛ a chɛ kɛ bɛlbwa nyin bofale dɛ́ ka so o kwaa ne.
translated: The deceit that Isaac and Jacob used to take his blessing was not good. They should have waited for him to give it willingly, but they chose to use trickery instead.

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,

translated: These are the Horite chiefs who lived in the land before the Edomites conquered it: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, and Anah,

translated: The sons of Ezer were Bilhan, Zaavan and Akan.

ref: Before there were kings in Israel, the following kings ruled Edom one after another: Bela son of Beor from Dinhabah; Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah; Husham from the land of Teman; Hadad son of Bedad from Avith ; Samlah from Masrekah; Shaul from the city of Rehoboth on the Euphrates River; Baalhanan son of Achbor; Hadar from the city of Pau .
text: Kooro Baal Hanan ɡe aa baa sewɛ la, Hadad chiɡ o naa ta, dɛ di kora. Dɛ o bwɛjen sɔne Pau. O haah ɡe sɔne Mɛhetabɛl, o ɛ Matrɛd bihaah. Matrɛd ɡe nya sɔne Mɛsahab.
translated: When Baal Hanan died, Hadad succeeded him as king. His city was named Pau. His wife's name was Mehetabel, she was Matred's daughter. Matred was the daughter of Me-Zahab.

ref: One day when Joseph's brothers had taken the sheep to a pasture near Shechem,
text: Awola bɛl, o dɔɔranar la kpɔ o mɛɛ kondɛɛra la kaa dibɔr Sɛkem pera,
translated: And he bought the piece of land where he had pitched his tent from the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father,

ref: “Don't murder him or even harm him. Just throw him into a well out here in the desert.” Reuben planned to rescue Joseph later and take him back to his father.

translated: At that time, "He is also there. He will see the work that they are doing." But if you don't tell them about it, they will see and think that you have come to deceive them.

ref: Hirah went back and told Judah, “I couldn't find the woman, and the people of Enaim said no prostitute had ever been there.”

translated: At that time Judah said to his brothers, "What profit is it if we kill our brother and conceal his blood?"

ref: When the brother with the red thread was born, they named him Zerah.

translated: When they reached the place where they were going, he acted as though he would go farther. But they urged him strongly, saying, "Stay with us." So he went in to stay with them, because it was already evening and the day was almost over.

text: Asɛ o bɔɔ ba ŋɔ, “Ɛrɛɛ he so sum ne ɡɛ nɔɔ?”

text: Tɔpetooro wee la, kooro ɛɛ lee dɛ́ berɛ kaa he ne he tombena ta. Ásɛ he kpɔ kooro numol do o nɔn ta kɛŋɔ he fa aa dɛ doo sen la.
translated: In the past, it was not easy to get salt at any time. Because if you wanted salt you had to go to the market to buy it before you could get it.

translated: When his brothers saw that their father loved Joseph more than all his brothers, they hated him and could not speak peacefully to him.

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: A habɔɔ tɔpetooro wee la, kooro la dɛ di o lola tɔpere, asɛ dɛ yerɛ o nomonome bwa. O le o sendoola nomoa la ne paanowɔɔra nomoa la, dɛ kpɔ ba ba o nɛra la sie.
translated: When a person is very sick, they should go to the hospital, so that they can examine their body. With their knowledge and medicine, they will find what is wrong with them.

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
text: O na bochaah la kɛŋɔ o wer. Asɛ o pere bochaah la le biro la bini,
translated: He is not a good person at all. Because he does bad things and lies,

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Asɛ o pa bɔr waa chɔ a baah ta la sɔn <nyundua>. Asɛ bɔr jo dɛ chaah. Onee tɔpere waa teŋa nɛɛ.
translated: The woman is cooking food to give to her husband <in the evening>. The woman is very happy. She loves her husband very much.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: Asɛ bɔr jo dɛ chaah, onee tɔpere waa teŋa tooro.
translated: The ground is very dry, so the plants are not growing well.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Anomanta, o ta nɛwɛnyina, ɡɛ tenten waa doa laanee o ta ba. Baa ne haahnee o ta.
translated: Truly, if you stand, you will be taller than your father. They will respect you.

ref: And I have given the green plants as food for everything else that breathes, including animals, both wild and tame, and birds. And so it was.

translated: they saw the young man and the old woman who were in the room, and the thing that they had done to their mother and they cried, they were very sad because it was their relative.>> So it was indeed.

ref: By the seventh day God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Tɔpenuanɛ te ne tela la, dɛ o tonɛ o toma bwa. Anomanta, o onɔ tɔpenuanɛ te la ta.
translated: The old woman is in her room, and she is cooking her food. However, she sees the old woman in the mirror.

ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: dɛ konduula ne kpɔɡa ta harɛ la ta leɛ, dɛkalkɛŋɔ dɛ o ta ɡelɛ bwaala nɛ o ta mɔɔ. Dɛ nomɛlbwa ɡe toole dɛ́ parɛ harɛ la.
translated: When a person is sick and goes to the hospital, they examine his body and his blood. The results show what is wrong with him.

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: Kɛ bɛ onɔ la waa dɛ tɛ liire dɛ́ ɡel nɛn jemɛ wedɛɛɡa ne anchɔɡa la te nɔn di. Awola haa di a ɡɛ daa la nɔn la bɛl la, sewaa hɛɛ ba sewa.”
translated: If someone comes to you and does not bring this teaching, do not receive him into your house or greet him. For whoever greets him shares in his evil works.

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,

translated: However, if you go and find that it is true, you should cut off your left ear and bring it.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Asɛ baa la ŋɔ, “Yɔɔ! Taraken te, n ɡbaɡba hoɡ ne n bo-ol tentenee nɔ. O sɔne ‘haah’. Baa binii o le.”
translated: "The father said, 'Yes! Tomorrow, I will make a big drum for you and you will beat it loudly. It will sound 'haah'. The father agreed to it.'"

ref: At once they saw what they had done, and they realized they were naked. Then they sewed fig leaves together to cover themselves.
text: Baa dia tonɛ la, buɡ la ta, ba bwa sie pilo, ba na kɛŋɔ ba cheɡɛ mumɡbal. Anomanta, ba nyin ɔɡ papooni dɛ nyara dɛ kpɔ li.
translated: When the day breaks, when the sun rises, they go to the farm, they work with their hoes. In the evening, they return home tired and hungry.

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: Mɛɛ kpɔ danchɔɡee ta he ne haah la baah ta, ásɛ he naanar ne o naanar hah dɔŋa dɛɛ la. Saŋ bɛlbwa waa nae la, wɛɛ paɡre he nyuu, ásɛ he ɡe choɡe o nalume.”
translated: When you see a stranger and give him water to drink, because his throat and your throat are not different. If the time comes, he will help you, because you have shown him kindness.

ref: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
text: Dɛ ŋɔ baa la be ŋɔ, “Haa no he haah nyoa dɛ di daa nɔn maa ŋɔa he bɛ di nomanta, N taah harɛ nɔ waa haa ɛa la wa, tomkpeɡaa hɛɛ ba ɛɛ dɛ́ kwee bɔwɔb mɔɔ dɛ́ di kwa he soɡa bini bwa.
translated: And they said to him, "If you allow us to go out, send us into the herd of pigs so that we may enter them."

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Korowii kpɔ ola tɔne tɛɛ ba ba kpɔ tɔ ba bwara. Korowii doŋi Adam ne Hawa le Eden bodɛɛɡe bela

ref: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?

text: Wedɛɛɡaa he ɛa, he sie ɛɛ lɛo. Kɛ haa ɛ anɔ la aa wola were la nomanta, wechɔɡa dɛ yoi. Kɛ a chɛ kɛŋɔ hé won le a ta.”
translated: When you go there, you will see it. If you want to know about their way of life, you should ask them. If you listen carefully, they will tell you.

ref: But Cain had to go far from the Lord and live in the Land of Wandering, which is east of Eden. More and More People
text: Belaa Keen le Korowii sie, dɛ ka so harɛ bɛl waa lɔ Eden wee leebɔr bobɛl baa dɛ yerɔ Nɔd. Keen naanar wa

translated: Zillah also bore a son, whose name was Tubal Cain. He was a metalworker. Zillah's daughter's name was Naamah.

translated: And Lamech said to his wives, "Adah and Zillah, hear my voice. Wives of Lamech, listen to what I say! I have killed a man for wounding me, a young man for striking me."

ref: Anyone who tries to get even with me will be punished ten times more than anyone who tries to get even with Cain.”
text: Dɛ nɛn kpo Keen, wɛɛ dɔ o wechɔɡa kembɔr bele anuanɛɛ yaa, nomɛlbwa waa kpo mɛ la, wɛɛ dɔ n wechɔɡa kembɔr dɔŋa bele fifraanuanɛ dɛ anuanɛɛ.” Sɛt ne Enɔs wa
translated: And also Cain, if anyone kills him, vengeance will be taken on him sevenfold, but if anyone kills my descendant Lamech, vengeance will be taken seventy-seven times." Seth and Enosh were born

ref: But the Lord was pleased with Noah,

text: Anomanta, Nowa, ka nyin daa waa kpeɡa, dɛ́ kwaa dakajen waa won nee sie vala kɛŋɔ ɡbera, ásɛ dɛ bɔne o bini kɛŋɔ deene, ásɛ dɛ kpɔ kootal maa o bini ne o habɔɔ bwa.
translated: Therefore, Noah, when you finish building, you should gather your wife and your children into the ark, because I will destroy the world, because people have become very bad and they do not listen to my word.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: N ne hɛɛ fɔwɛ fɔwee, ásɛ he kpɔ he haah ne he bibaala, ne ba ɡe haahna bwa jo daka la bini dɛ́ ja he nɛ.
translated: And when it was evening, they brought to him many who were possessed with demons, and he cast out the spirits with a word and healed all who were sick.

ref: Noah took a male and a female of every living creature with him,

translated: When the flood came, it covered the whole earth so that the tops of the highest mountains were under water. Only Noah and his family were safe in the ark.

ref: He waited seven more days before sending the dove out again, and this time it did not return.

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.

text: a taa mɛ ba fɔo la n ne hen ne hweera kwaa bwa soo siwi aa fɔwa la nae. Aa won ba kɛ nee ɛɛ ba suu dɛ́ ba chɔɡ kontaale bɛlbwa kpa.
translated: and they did not come at all and there was no noise or anything that was heard. They just stayed until they finished building their mud houses.

ref: The crops failed, and there was no food anywhere in Canaan. So Abram and his wife Sarai went to live in Egypt for a while. But just before they got there, Abram said, “Sarai, you are really beautiful!

translated: When Abram arrived in the land of Canaan, he and Lot separated because their possessions were too many, and Lot went to live in the Jordan valley near Sodom.

ref: After Abram and Lot had gone their separate ways, the Lord said to Abram: Look around to the north, south, east, and west.

translated: When Abram and Lot separated from each other, the Lord said to Abram, "Lift up your eyes and look from where you are, to the north and to the south."

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: A ɡɛ harɛ nɔ bwaa mɛɛ ba kpɔe tɛ he ne he naanar ó den ɛ he kon saŋ bɛlbwa dɛɛ la.
translated: They will continue to do this until they see that their father has given them their share of the inheritance.

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.

translated: So he moved his tent and went and settled near Mamre, which is in Hebron. And he built an altar there to the Lord.

ref: King Chedorlaomer and his allies had ruled these last five kings for twelve years, but in the thirteenth year the kings rebelled and joined forces in Siddim Valley, which is now covered by the southern part of the Dead Sea.
text: Bene fi dɛ anɛ bwa dɛ ba lɔ kooro Kedolaoma lɔɡa dɛɛ tomɔ, asɛ bene fi dɛ atoro ta la, ba ɛɔ dɔm ŋɔ baa on tomɛ kpa.
translated: They did not want to go to Kedolaoma's house again, because they were afraid that if they went, they would not find him at home again.

ref: They also defeated the Horites in the hill country of Edom, as far as El-Paran, near the desert.

translated: They turned back and came to Kadesh, which is also called En Mishpat. They conquered all the territory of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites who were living in Hazazon Tamar.

text: Abram berɔ lɛ laala la bwa, ne o lole Lɔt ne o laala ne o haahna baa laɡ ba ja o nɛɛ la, dɛ nɛra bwa baa laɡ ba ja la, dɛ ba bane. Mɛlkesɛdɛk swee andɛɛɡa tɛ Abram
translated: Abram took his wife and went, and he took Lot with his wife and all their possessions with him, and the people who were with them, and they left. Melchizedek blessed Abraham

translated: When Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him returned, they came back. On their way back, they defeated the king of Sodom at the Valley of Shaveh. That place is called the King's Valley.

translated: "The Lord says, 'If you return to me with all your heart, and remove the foreign gods and the Ashtoreths from among you, and prepare your hearts for the Lord, and serve him only.'"

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: O berɔ chwa la o naleɡ bini, dɛ ka lawɛ nao bie o bwara aa wera, dɛ kpɔ ba tɛ o bonɔɔ bɛl, ásɛ o ɛ warane kpoo dɛ́ daalo.
translated: When a woman is pregnant, if she doesn't take care of herself properly, and she gets sick, it will affect the baby, because she and the baby are one.

ref: So she laughed and said to herself, “Now that I am worn out and my husband is old, will I really know such happiness?”
text: Anomanta, o mamɛ o nyu bini ŋɔ, “Taraken n ne n jaa bwa aa hwɛɛhra tonɛ nɔɔ mɛɛ ba silɛɛ kon nɔ dɔŋ dɔe yaa?”
translated: Then, when his mother saw him, she said, "Why did you come back so early when the sun has not yet set?"

ref: “Don't get in our way,” the crowd answered. “You're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.” The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
text: Anee ba ŋɔ, “Haa waa nyin aa, le ya sie. Hɔɔhree hen bonɔ, henee dɛ́ won baɡle yaa wa aa? He ta bela leɛ he kwaa ɛɛ ɡwaa ba kwaa.” Belaa ba ka lɔ tɔ o nɛ, dɛ́ fe taɡɔ le bela, dɛ́ dɔ bɔr ka ku ɡbera la.
translated: They said to him, "If you are who you say you are, then prove it. Why should we believe what you are saying? Show us a sign so we can all see it." But when they asked him this, he refused to show them a sign, saying that no sign would be given to this generation.

ref: But the two angels in the house reached out and pulled Lot safely inside.
text: Anee baala banɛ la aa na ɡɛ la, ba ha nɔn lawɛ Lɔt jo buse la bini, dɛ laa ɡbera tɔ.
translated: When those men saw that they were not able to do anything to Lot, they struck them with blindness.

ref: Hurry! Run! I can't do anything until you are safely there.” The town was later called Zoar because Lot had said it was small. Sodom and Gomorrah Are Destroyed
text: Ɛ warane, dɛ́ chwa kaa. Dɛ he ta bela telaa, maa won ɛ wobɛlbwa.” A waa ba pa bwɛɛ la sɔn Soar.
translated: And he said to him, "See, I have granted you this request too; I will not overthrow the town you speak of." Hurry, escape there, because I cannot do anything until you arrive there." That is why the town was called Zoar.

ref: The next day the older daughter said to her sister, “I slept with my father last night. We'll get him drunk again tonight, so you can sleep with him, and we can each have a child.”
text: Tɔ aa pera la, o bol o nime la be ŋɔ, “N ne n mɛɛ chɔɔ dɛ jwaana ne. Ɛrɛ ɡel ya tɛo ane jwaana nɔ ne ɡe ja, ásɛ he ɡe ne o chɔ. Ásɛ a che ya mɛɛ nɛɛ ya mun bɛ he.”
translated: When the time came, he called his wife and said, "I and my friend are going on a journey. If anything happens to me on this journey, take care of him. Because he will take care of you. Because you two don't know each other yet."

ref: The younger daughter named her son Benammi, and he is the ancestor of the Ammonites.
text: Bechala la ɡe lolɛ bibaa, asɛ dɛ pa o sɔn Bɛn Ami. O ɡee Amɔn tena bwa naa.
translated: The younger also bore a son, and called his name Ben-Ammi. He is the father of the Ammonites to this day.

ref: Then God continued: I know you are innocent. That's why I kept you from sleeping with Sarah and doing anything wrong.
text: Belaa Korowii ŋɔ o be deera ta ŋɔ, “Kpelaa. N ɡbabir jemɔ kɛŋɔ liichɔɡa laa he lii. Menee lawɛ kpoŋe, ásɛ he bɛ n nɛɛ jo. A waa n ta ɡelɛ he kpɔ he te leɡo la.
translated: When Korowi heard this, he said, "Thank you. My friend, I have been looking for work for a long time. I am very happy because you have helped me. You have given me something good that will help me in life."

ref: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.
text: O bie Aesek aa dɔ yawanyoa la, o teŋe o pen ɡɛ Korowii aa ŋɔ o be la.
translated: When you reach Aesek village, turn right to Korowii where he is.

ref: One day, Sarah noticed Hagar's son Ishmael playing, and she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that Egyptian slave woman and her son! I don't want him to inherit anything. It should all go to my son.”
text: Belaa o ŋɔ Abraham be ŋɔ, “Doŋi yomhaah la ne o bie la. Dɛkalkɛŋɔ yomhaah la bie ne n bie Aesek aa won pɛ di he kapɛrɛ a maɡre.”
translated: "Then he said to Abraham, "Take your son, your only son, whom you love—Isaac—and go to the region of Moriah. Sacrifice him there as a burnt offering on a mountain I will show you.""

ref: Early the next morning Abraham gave Hagar an animal skin full of water and some bread. Then he put the boy on her shoulder and sent them away. They wandered around in the desert near Beersheba,
text: Anomanta, Abraham iro chɔmbibii dɛ kpɔ kondikwaa ne oltɔn pur, dɛ do nee o bini o su dɛ kpɔ chɔŋɛ Heeɡa vaama ta. O lawɛ bie la ja o nɛ, dɛ ta ba ŋwɛnɛ ba kaa. Heeɡa aa lea kaa la, bobɛl waa ɛ pua hɛŋle ɡbo harɛ baa yerɔ Beɛseebaa o ka do dɛɛ ɡaaɡe.
translated: However, Abraham complained to Abimelech about a well of water that Abimelech's servants had seized. He had not known about it, and they had not told him. God was not pleased with this, so he sent them both away from Beersheba to go to their own places.

ref: When God heard the boy crying, the angel of God called out to Hagar from heaven and said, “Hagar, why are you worried? Don't be afraid. I have heard your son crying.

text: Jekɔb ta ɡelɛ Laban jemɛ kɛŋɔ o dɛ kaa o bwɛ.
translated: Jacob took Laban's daughter without his father knowing.

ref: and said: Look what you've done! You've tricked me and run off with my daughters like a kidnapper.
text: Laban la Jekɔb be dɛ ka ŋɔ, “Ɛrɛɛ he ɛɛ mɛ ɡɛ nɔɔ? He neɡ mɛ dɛ kpɔ n bihaahna chwa kɛŋɔ haa kaa yo laa dɛ laɡ ba la.
translated: Laban said to Jacob, "What is this you have done? You have deceived me and carried off my daughters like captives in war."

ref: I can understand why you were eager to return to your father, but why did you have to steal my idols?
text: N jemɔ kɛŋɔ he sie cheɡɛ chiŋ hɛɛ nyin bɔr dɛ́ kaa he bwɛ. Aah dɛ ɛrɛ waa he berɛ ŋwɛ n voɡa nɔɔ?”
translated: "And what kind of work is it that you do that you don't have time to come and greet? Or is it that you don't want to see my face again?"

ref: If you find that any one of us has taken your idols, I'll have that person killed. Let your relatives be witnesses. Show me what belongs to you, and you can take it back.” Jacob did not realize that Rachel had stolen the household idols.
text: Nyiŋi, nomɛlbwa haa na voɡa la o be la, a chɛ kɛ bá kpoo. Ya nɛra nɔ ɡe lɔ bonɔ dɛ́ di yaa danseɛ, komɛlbwa waa ɛ he kon la, ásɛ he kpɔ.” Dɛ Jekɔb ta jemɛ kɛŋɔ Rakɛlee ŋwɛ o mɛɛ Laban voɡa la.
translated: Now, when Rachel saw that she bore Jacob no children, Rachel envied her sister; and said unto Jacob, "Give me children, or else I die." And Jacob's anger was kindled against Rachel: and he said, "Am I in God's stead, who hath withheld from thee the fruit of the womb?"

ref: Laban searched the tents of Jacob, Leah, and the two servant women, but did not find the idols. Then he went to Rachel's tent.
text: Laban kaa jo Jekɔb tɛnte dea bini dɛ ka ɡaaɡe, dɛ le ka jo Lea ne o toli la ɡe kon bini o ta voɡa la nae. Belaa o berɛ la Rakɛl ɡe kon bini.
translated: When Jacob had stayed with Laban for a month, Laban said to him that he should not work for him for nothing just because he was his relative. Then he asked Rachel what he wanted as payment.

ref: Rachel said, “Father, please don't be angry with me for not getting up; I'm having my period.” Laban kept on searching, but still did not find the idols.
text: Asɛ Rakɛl ŋɔ o mɛɛ be ŋɔ, “N poori n mɛɛ, bɛ ɡel he baah di. Maa won ir che he sie, dɛkalkɛ n le dahara.” Laban nyiŋi o tɛnte dea bini kɛ wɛɛ na voɡa laanee, kɛ o ta an nae.
translated: Then Rachel said to her father, "Do not be angry my lord, that I cannot rise up before you, for the custom of women is upon me." And he searched but did not find the household gods.

ref: If a wild animal killed one of your sheep or goats, I paid for it myself. In fact, you demanded the full price, whether the animal was killed during the day or at night.
text: Anɔ la konchɔɡa aa dɛ kpoora bwa la, n ɡbabirii dɛɛ bɔe a kem. Anɔ la baa dɛ ŋwɛa webaŋa ne jwaana ɡe bwa la, he ɡelɔ mɛɛ bɔe a kem.
translated: If a person's heart is not clean, their prayers will not be answered. If a person does not forgive others and reconcile with them, God will not answer their prayers.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Dɛ berɛ ŋɔ, “Dɛ he ka dɛ fiiɡi n bihaahna nɔ kɛɛ dɛ ka mo haahna ja ba nɛ aa, a lole kɛŋɔ maa belaa do te, kɛ Korowii te ɛɛ nao.
translated: At that time, "If we do not repent and turn from our evil ways, punishment will surely come upon us, as the Lord has said."

ref: Early the next morning, Laban kissed his daughters and his grandchildren goodbye, then he left to go back home.
text: Tɔ aa pera chɔmbibi la, Laban mo o bihaahna ne o naanar la lwɛrɛme, dɛ swee andɛɛɡa tɛɛ ba dɛ berɛ kaa o bwɛ.
translated: When Laban heard the news about his sister's son, he ran to meet him, embraced him, kissed him and brought him to his house.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Belaa Jekɔb tomɛ toroma ŋɔ bá tɔ o sie dɛ́ kaa o dɔɔ Esɔ be. Dɛ Esɔ soɡe harɛ bɛl baa dɛ yerɛ bela Edom.
translated: Before Jacob's descendants became a nation, his brother Esau had already established the nation of Edom.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”
text: Kondɛɛra waa peɡri a bini la, o ŋɔ bá kpɔ ka tɛ o dɔɔ, a bɛlbwa kapa ne nɛn waa dɛ nyiŋa. Asɛ dɛ ŋɔ o bonɔɔna la be ŋɔ, “Hé tɔ n sie, ásɛ dɛ ɡel he baahna tɛr.”
translated: When the hyena saw that the child was born, and that he could not eat it, he became very angry and went away. But before he left, he said, "Hey you woman, when the child grows up, tell him about me."

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: he chaŋe ŋɔ, ‘He bonɔɔ Jekɔb kwaa. O lawa ŋɔ ń kpɔ ka tɛ o dɔɔ Esɔ. O ɡe lɔ mun ta dɛɛ ba.’ ”
translated: "He said, 'Your name will no longer be Jacob. Your name from now on will be Israel because you have wrestled with God and with men and have prevailed.'"

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Gɛ tentenee o ŋɔ nɛn nɛɛte, ne toorote, ne banɔ la bwa waa kpɔ kondɛɛra do ba nɔn ta la be ŋɔ, “Dɛ he cheme Esɔ aa, hé bwa ɡe ŋɔ o be ɡɛ.
translated: And truly if you see it, with its trees, and its beauty and things that are there, you will say, "If this is not heaven, then it is close to it."

ref: Jacob got up in the middle of the night and took his wives, his eleven children, and everything he owned across to the other side of the Jabbok River for safety.
text: Waa kpɔ ba le nee la tonɛ la, o berɛ ba kpɔ komɛlbwa ɡe waa dɔa la ɡe le,
translated: When you see people in the world, sometimes you see some who are very poor,

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: asɛ dɛ ɡbo ka baŋ bela. Belaa baa bɛl ba jwiiru, ba kpoŋe dɔŋa bini awola jwaana la ne, dɛ ka tel kɛŋɔ aa baa ka tama ásɛ bɔr chaah.
translated: The reason they came to the village. When they came, they asked about their friend, whether he had reached home safely, and they said that they wanted to see him very much.

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Dɛ kpoŋe ba le fini Israel tena aa ol kɛɡra di. Baa la aa kpɔ nɔn lɔ Jekɔb kɛɡra ta o sor la nomanta.
translated: They did not find any fault in Israel. They did not see any evil in Jacob's descendants or his children.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Jekɔb chiɡ ŋwɛnɛ ta dɛɛ kaa, asɛ nyundua toroma ba chemo.
translated: Jacob also married their younger sister, because he loved her very much.

ref: He thought, “If Esau attacks one group, perhaps the other can escape.”
text: O liiro kɛŋɔ dɛ Esɔ ba dɛ yo baŋ bɛl nɛra aa, ba kar la ɛɛ won dɔ bɔr dɛ́ chwa kaa.
translated: When Esau's wives saw that they did not please his father, they were very bitter in their hearts.

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Jekɔb chɔ bela awola jwaana la bwa, asɛ dɛ chale o kondɛɛra la bini ane dɛ́ kpɔ ka tɛ Esɔ.
translated: Jacob took two of his wives and his children and his possessions and crossed the river to go meet Esau.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Hé berɛ ɡel ó jemɛ kɛŋɔ o bonɔɔ Jekɔb ɡe lɔ habɔɔ dɛɛ ba. Dɛ ń tɔ sie, dɛ́ kpɔ koŋkɛɛla nɔ ba tɛɛ, ásɛ mɔɔ o tel he be. Ásɛ dɛ he nao waa, he ken ɛɛ kpɔɔ kɛɔ.”
translated: When the time came for him to die, he called his son Jacob and said to him, "If I have found favor in your eyes, put your hand under my thigh and promise that you will show me kindness and faithfulness. Do not bury me in Egypt."

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: O ŋɔ o bonɔɔ waa tɔ sie la be ŋɔ, “Dɛ n dɔɔ Esɔ cheme dɛ bɔɔre ŋɔ he dɔɔree umeo? Leperaa hɛɛ kaa lɔ? Ume kondɛɛraa he kpɔ dɛɛ kaa laa,
translated: And if his son asks him saying, "What shall I do to inherit eternal life? What is the law? What does the commandment say,"

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Gɛ tentenee o ŋɔ nɛn nɛɛte, ne toorote, ne banɔ la bwa waa kpɔ kondɛɛra do ba nɔn ta la be ŋɔ, “Dɛ he cheme Esɔ aa, hé bwa ɡe ŋɔ o be ɡɛ.
translated: And truly if you see it, with its fruits, and its kingdom, you will see things that will make you say, "This is indeed God's place, you will see it and be amazed."

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Baa la aa baa na kɛŋɔ waa won ɡo Jekɔb la, o kpɔ nɔn lɔ Jekɔb kɛɡra asɛ o sor.
translated: When they arrived at their father's house to Jacob, he saw that Jacob was still alive.

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Jekɔb aa le Pɛneɛl dɛɛ kaa la dɛ wee leɔ. O kɛɡra la aa sora nomanta, dɛ o dɛ chole o ta.
translated: Jacob left Peniel and continued on his journey. He was limping because of his hip.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Belaa Jekɔb tomɛ toroma ŋɔ bá tɔ o sie dɛ́ kaa o dɔɔ Esɔ be. Dɛ Esɔ soɡe harɛ bɛl baa dɛ yerɛ bela Edom.
translated: Jacob's descendants became a nation and they were called Israel. Esau also had many descendants and they were called Edom.

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Jekɔb aa no ɡɛ la, o ɔmɔ kohona o bwara yɔr, asɛ dɛ bɔne o nɛra la ne peera ne nae, dɛ nyoɡmare la bini bɔre anɛ.
translated: When Jacob saw him, he tore his clothes in grief, because he loved him more than all his children, and mourned for him many days.

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Jekɔb chɔ bela awola jwaana la bwa, asɛ dɛ chale o kondɛɛra la bini ane dɛ́ kpɔ ka tɛ Esɔ.
translated: Jacob took two of his wives and his children and his possessions and crossed the river to go meet Esau.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Hé berɛ ɡel ó jemɛ kɛŋɔ o bonɔɔ Jekɔb ɡe lɔ habɔɔ dɛɛ ba. Dɛ ń tɔ sie, dɛ́ kpɔ koŋkɛɛla nɔ ba tɛɛ, ásɛ mɔɔ o tel he be. Ásɛ dɛ he nao waa, he ken ɛɛ kpɔɔ kɛɔ.”
translated: When the time came for her to give birth, there were indeed twins in her womb. The first one came out red, and his whole body was like a hairy garment; so they named him Esau.

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: he chaŋe ŋɔ, ‘He bonɔɔ Jekɔb kwaa. O lawa ŋɔ ń kpɔ ka tɛ o dɔɔ Esɔ. O ɡe lɔ mun ta dɛɛ ba.’ ”
translated: "He said, 'His name is not Jacob. His name that I will call him is that he is Esau. He will be the first to come out.'"

ref: Jacob got up in the middle of the night and took his wives, his eleven children, and everything he owned across to the other side of the Jabbok River for safety.
text: Waa kpɔ ba le nee la tonɛ la, o berɛ ba kpɔ komɛlbwa ɡe waa dɔa la ɡe le,
translated: When they saw that the matter was serious, at that time they saw the chief's wife who was coming,

ref: “No!” Jacob said. “Please accept them as a sign of your friendship for me. When you welcomed me and I saw your face, it was like seeing the face of God.
text: Anee Jekɔb ɡe ŋɔ, “Akai, n dɛ soŋe dɛ he sie lɛ n nɛɛ tenaa he kpɔ. Taraken maa nae nɔ, a denɔ ɛɛ mɛ kɛŋɔ maa na Korowii ken.
translated: Then Jacob said, "Father, I am not refusing to give it, but I want to know first. Perhaps when you die, your children will say that the money belongs to Korowi."

ref: Shechem even asked his father to arrange for him to marry her.
text: Sɛkem ŋɔ o mɛɛ be ŋɔ, “N mɛɛ, n dɛ nyin kɛŋɔ he ka nyin Daena tɛ mɛ ń mo.”
translated: "If you love me, you will keep my commandments."

ref: Meanwhile, Jacob heard what had happened. But his sons were out in the fields with the cattle, so he did not do anything at the time.
text: Dɛ Jekɔb noɔ kɛŋɔ Sɛkem ɡuɡri o bihaah Daena o ne o chɔ, kɛ bela la, o ta wobɛlbwa chaŋɛ, dɛkalkɛ dɛ o bibaala la kaa nae ne dibɔr ba ta balɛ mɔɔ.
translated: When Jacob arrived safely at the city of Shechem in the land of Canaan, on his journey from Paddan Aram, he camped before the city.

ref: just as Jacob's sons were coming in from work. When they learned that their sister had been raped, they became furiously angry, because nothing is more disgraceful than rape, and it must not be tolerated.
text: dɛ Jekɔb bii la ɡe denɔ le kpɔɡ bini ba. Baa baa no waa la la, a we ba nɛ, asɛ ba baah di kohona kɛŋɔ Sɛkem aa ɛ wedoŋa dɛ nyiŋi Israel tena nyubaah kɛŋɔ waa ɡuɡri ba nimambie o ne o chɔ la.
translated: and Jacob's children were twelve sons. Their names were, he gave them, because they were born in the land of Shechem and he named them after the twelve tribes of Israel according to their mothers and their births.

ref: Why don't you start letting your families marry into our families and ours marry into yours?
text: Gel ya nyoa pɛ yaa mo he bihaahna, ásɛ he ɡe ɛɛ mo ya kwa.
translated: Go and tell your father that he is a fool, because he gave you to me as a wife.

ref: Shechem added, “Do this favor for me, and I'll give whatever you want.
text: Asɛ Sɛkem ŋɔ Daena mɛɛ ne o dɔɔranar be ŋɔ, “Hɛ́ɛ sɛoh dɛ́ ɛ ɡɛ tɛ mɛ naa, komɛlbwa haa lɛ la, mɛɛ tɛ heyaa.
translated: Then Shechem said to Dinah's father, "If I have found favor in your eyes, whatever you ask of me, I will give."

ref: Just think! We'll get their property, as well as their flocks and herds. All we have to do is to agree, and they will live here with us.
text: A ba ɡɛ naa, ba kondɛɛra ne komɛlbwa aa ya kwaa ɛa? Hé ɡel ya sɛh ásɛ ya ne ba so.”
translated: "When they come, will they find us with our lamps lit or not? That is what he is asking us."

ref: They answered, “Was it right to let our own sister be treated that way?”
text: Anee ba chaŋe ŋɔ, “Yaa won so nyiŋi ba ɛ ya nimambie kɛŋɔ hanjwamane.”
translated: "They said to him, "You are not yet fifty years old and you have seen Abraham?"

ref: While Jacob and his family were traveling through Canaan, God terrified the people in the towns so much that no one dared bother them.
text: Jekɔb ne o bii aa dɛ ba bela ira la, Korowii ɡelɔ ɔmee kohona chole nɛra baa lɔ bwɛɛre aa bɛne bela tena bwa nɛ ba ta wone ti ba dɛ ɛɛ ba wobɛlbwa.
translated: Jacob and his children who were going with him, God listed their names one by one so that we would know those who went with him to the land they were traveling to.

ref: Rachel was at the point of death, and right before dying, she said, “I'll name him Benoni.” But Jacob called him Benjamin.
text: Dɛ Rakɛl dɛ ba sewa, anomanta, o pa bie la sɔn Bɛn Oni. Bɛn Oni munii n wenaale bie. Asɛ o mɛɛ ɡe pa o sɔn Bɛnjamen. Bɛnjamen munii n nɔndiɡ bie.
translated: And Rachel also, however, she did not give birth to a son Ben Oni. Ben Oni means son of sorrow. But her husband gave him a son Benjamin. Benjamin means son of the right hand.

ref: During their time there, Jacob's oldest son Reuben slept with Bilhah, who was one of Jacob's other wives. And Jacob found out about it. Jacob's Twelve Sons
text: Waa kaa so harɛ la ta la, Rubin kaa lawɛ tolo baa dɛ yerɔ Bilha baa lawa tɛ o mɛɛ o ɛ o sɛno la chɔ, asɛ Jekɔb no. Bii fi dɛ banɛɛ Jekɔb lolɛ.
translated: While they were living there, Reuben went and slept with his father's concubine Bilhah, and Israel heard about it. Jacob had twelve sons.

ref: He did this because the land was too crowded and could not support him and his brother with their flocks and herds.
text: Dɛkalkɛ dɛ harɛ o ne Jekɔb aa soɡe o ta la aa kanɛ tɛɛ ba. Ba dɔ kondɛɛra asɛ a ya, anomanta, dɛ a chɛ kɛ bɛlbwa nyin bofale dɛ́ ka so o kwaa ne.
translated: The deceitful thing that Jacob did to his brother was not good at all. They should have discussed it peacefully, but instead, he chose to use trickery which was not right at all.

ref: All of these clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom.
text: Dɛ a ɡɛ nɛra nɔ bwa ɛ Esɔ bibaala, dɛ bwa ɛ koranar.
translated: They went to the place where Esau lived, they went to meet him.

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Sɛɛ waa ɛ Hɔr te bibaala waa lol ba asɛ ba so Edom laanee, Lotan ne Sobal ne Sibeon dɛ Ana,
translated: These are the Horite chiefs who lived in the land before the Edomites conquered it: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, and Anah,

ref: Ezer's sons were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
text: Asɛ Esa ɡe bibaalaa, Bilhan ne Saavan dɛ Akan.
translated: The sons of Ezer were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.

ref: Before there were kings in Israel, the following kings ruled Edom one after another: Bela son of Beor from Dinhabah; Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah; Husham from the land of Teman; Hadad son of Bedad from Avith ; Samlah from Masrekah; Shaul from the city of Rehoboth on the Euphrates River; Baalhanan son of Achbor; Hadar from the city of Pau .
text: Kooro Baal Hanan ɡe aa baa sewɛ la, Hadad chiɡ o naa ta, dɛ di kora. Dɛ o bwɛjen sɔne Pau. O haah ɡe sɔne Mɛhetabɛl, o ɛ Matrɛd bihaah. Matrɛd ɡe nya sɔne Mɛsahab.
translated: When Baal Hanan died, Hadad succeeded him as king. His city was named Pau. His wife's name was Mehetabel, she was Matred's daughter. Matred was the daughter of Me-zahab.

ref: One day, Joseph told his brothers what he had dreamed, and they hated him even more.
text: Awola jwaana bɛl Jekɔb deero dɛ taa baɡle o dɔɔranar. A taa ba haoh kohona kwara.
translated: The young man Jacob loved was very beautiful. He went to work for her father for seven years.

ref: One day when Joseph's brothers had taken the sheep to a pasture near Shechem,
text: Awola bɛl, o dɔɔranar la kpɔ o mɛɛ kondɛɛra la kaa dibɔr Sɛkem pera,
translated: And when he saw that he could not prevail against him, he touched the hollow of his thigh; and the hollow of Jacob's thigh was out of joint,

ref: his father Jacob said to him, “I want you to go to your brothers. They are with the sheep near Shechem.” “Yes, sir,” Joseph answered.
text: asɛ Jekɔb ŋɔ Josef be ŋɔ, “He dɔɔranar kpɔ kondɛɛra kaa dibɔr Sɛkem pera, n dɛ́ tomɛ ba be.” Asɛ o chaŋe ŋɔ, “N mɛɛ, mɛɛ lao.”
translated: Then Jacob said to Joseph, "Your brothers are herding/tending the sheep at Shechem, I will send you to them." And he answered him, "I am here, I am ready to go."

ref: His father said, “Go and find out how your brothers and the sheep are doing. Then come back and let me know.” So he sent him from Hebron Valley. Joseph was near Shechem
text: O mɛɛ ŋɔ o be ŋɔ, “La dɛ́ ka na he dɔɔranar ne ɡɛ kondɛɛra la aa doa, ásɛ dɛ ba bol n be.” O tomɔ le Hɛbrɔn Fɛlembɔjen bɔr pera dɛ kaa Sɛkem.
translated: And when he arrived, he said, "Go and see how your brothers and the flocks are doing, and bring me word." So he sent him from the Valley of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.

ref: “Don't murder him or even harm him. Just throw him into a well out here in the desert.” Reuben planned to rescue Joseph later and take him back to his father.
text: Dɛ berɛ ŋɔ, “He bɛ on kpo. Hé kpɔɔ ta kɔlɔŋ nɔ bɛl bini.” Gɛ aa ɛ o won lɛo le ba nɔn ta, dɛ́ kpɔɔ ka tɛ ba mɛɛ la waa o bol ɡɛ.
translated: At that time, "He is with you. He will show you a large upper room furnished." And when you enter there, prepare, for you will eat the Passover there with him.

ref: Jacob mourned for Joseph a long time, and to show his sorrow he tore his clothes and wore sackcloth.
text: Belaa Jekɔb loo ku o laa o batakaare kɛɛ, dɛ kpɔ lweyal tɔ dɛ ɛ o bie la lwe dɛ la sie.
translated: Jacob took his staff and went to his relatives, and he worked there for seven years to marry his wife.

ref: and they had three sons. He named the first one Er;
text: Juda ne haah la kpoŋe tea dɛ lolɛ bibaa baa yerɔ Ɛr.
translated: The Jews did not want to stone him because he was doing good works.

ref: The third one was born when Judah was in Chezib, and she named him Shelah.
text: O berɔ lolɛ bibaa ja, asɛ dɛ pa o sɔn Seela. Gɛ saŋ baa lolɔ la, dɛ ba soɡe bwɛɛ bɛl baa dɛ yerɔ Kesib.
translated: When she was giving birth to him, she called his name Shelah. He was at Kezib when she bore him.

ref: The Lord wasn't pleased with Onan and took his life too.
text: Korowii be la, dɛ a ɡɛ liire nɔ ɛ wechɔɡa, asɛ dɛ kpo o ɡe.
translated: The woman said that she will go to the market tomorrow, if it doesn't rain.

ref: Hirah went back and told Judah, “I couldn't find the woman, and the people of Enaim said no prostitute had ever been there.”
text: Asɛ o berɛ la Juda be dɛ ka ŋɔ, “Maa laŋa la, n ta on nae, asɛ dɛ bɔɔ baala bane baa lɔ bela o wa, ba ɡe ŋɔ hanjwamane bɛlbwa toole bela.”
translated: "At that time Judah said to his brothers, "What profit is it if we kill our brother and conceal his blood?"

ref: When the brother with the red thread was born, they named him Zerah.
text: Dɛ berɛ lolɛ onɔ la jiseswɛm aa vɔwɛ o nɔn la. Asɛ ba pa o sɔn Sɛra.
translated: At that time the child was not yet born. So they named him Sarah.

ref: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Ismael tena la kpɔ Josef kaa Ijipt, dɛ ka yoɡu tɛ Pɔtefa waa ɛ Ijipt kooro nɛra baa dɛ daahno la nomonome la bɛl.
translated: Ishmael's descendants sold Joseph to Egypt, and he became a slave in the house of Potiphar, one of Egypt's great officials.

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: O nomoa la ɡe nao kɛŋɔ Korowii teŋɛ o nɛ dɛ ɡel o wobɛlbwa do wer tɛo.
translated: He went and stayed in the Korowi area and he was not able to return home again.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Pɔtefa kpɔ komɛlbwa do o nɔn ta dɛ laa liire kpɔ a ta. Kwaa waa dɛ lii a waa kondikwa waa di ɡbo. Dɛ Josef mao kɛrɛdɛ dɛ do wer bonɔɔ ɡe.
translated: The potter saw the clay pot in his hand and he was very happy. Because he had made it with his own hands. So Joseph was very grateful and gave thanks to God.

ref: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
text: Awola bɛl, Josef lɔ dem bela dɛɛ ɛ o toma. Dɛ dem bela bonɔɔna la bɛlbwa toole.
translated: When night came, Joseph took the body and wrapped it. Then they wrapped the body in a clean linen cloth.

ref: Potiphar's wife grabbed hold of his coat and said, “Make love to me!” Joseph ran out of the house, leaving his coat there in her hands.
text: Belaa haah la ta lawɛ o yal kpoŋe dɛ ŋɔ, “Gel n ne he chɔ.” O ta sɛɛh, dɛ yo lɛ o te dɛ chwa le buse, asɛ a ka o yal la do haah la nɔn ta.
translated: When they saw that he was not going to die, they changed their minds and said, "He is a god." He did not die, so they thought he must be a god, because the snake that bit him was very poisonous.

ref: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
text: dɛ ɡel ba ka lawɛ Josef ba do dea, bela kooro aa lawɛ nɛra dɛɛ do la, asɛ o chɔ bela.
translated: When they went to sell Joseph to the merchants, his brother Reuben was not there, because he had gone somewhere else.

ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: asɛ dɛ laɡ ba do dea. Nomoa waa chɔ nɛra baa dɛ daah kooro dem la, o deemii o kpɔ ba do. A ɡɛ dea la binii ba lawɛ Josef ɡe do.
translated: The matter was very difficult. But when they saw that their father was determined to do it, they agreed to take him along. So they took their brother Joseph along with them.

ref: and he asked, “Why are you so worried today?”
text: Asɛ o bɔɔ ba ŋɔ, “Ɛrɛɛ he so sum ne ɡɛ nɔɔ?”
translated: "Why did you come here?"

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three branches stand for three days,
text: Anee Josef ŋɔ o be ŋɔ, “Kenii he deera la mun ɛɛ baɡle. Daa kpala atoro la cheɡɔɔ tɛ tɔpera atoro.
translated: And Joseph said to them, "Don't be afraid, am I in the place of God? You intended evil against me.

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: Tɔpetooro wee la, kooro ɛɛ lee dɛ́ berɛ kaa he ne he tombena ta. Ásɛ he kpɔ kooro numol do o nɔn ta kɛŋɔ he fa aa dɛ doo sen la.
translated: In the past, a man would not marry just any woman. Because if he saw a woman he liked, he would first have to work for her family.

ref: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
text: Paanowɔɔra nomoa la aa noa kɛŋɔ Josef wono baɡle o dɔŋ la deera la mun tɛo a do som la, o bol o be ŋɔ, “N ɡe deero dɛ che paanoo wɛɡrɛre atoro.
translated: When his brothers saw that their father loved Joseph more than all his brothers, they hated him and could not speak peacefully to him.

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: A habɔɔ tɔpetooro wee la, kooro la dɛ di o lola tɔpere, asɛ dɛ yerɛ o nomonome bwa. O le o sendoola nomoa la ne paanowɔɔra nomoa la, dɛ kpɔ ba ba o nɛra la sie.
translated: When you are a leader, you should not be proud, but rather be humble. You should listen to the advice of your elders and the advice of your peers, so that they will respect you.

ref: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
text: asɛ dɛ teŋe paanowɔɔra nomoa la nyu ɡɛ Josef aa bola la.
translated: because the land belonged to Pharaoh alone except for what the priests had

ref: Suddenly, seven fat, healthy cows came up from the river and started eating grass along the bank.
text: o na nae anuanɛ aa do bwara, dɛ le moo la ta dɛ baa chwe kpɔɡa o nyoa.
translated: and if you do not have money, you can come and work to pay for your treatment.

ref: Later, seven other heads of grain appeared, but they were thin and scorched by a wind from the desert.
text: Asɛ anfala anuanɛ ɡe berɛ sɛɡle daa la bampera. Peuwɔb bɛl tao le wee leebɔr dɛ ba chɔɡ a te, a ta wone ta pure.
translated: The ground was very dry because it had not rained for a long time. People were worried that if rain did not come soon, they would not be able to farm.
